tickets.title: Clarify Course Catalog Usability Requirements
tickets.description:
Description
Clarify what stakeholders mean by "the course catalog is ugly" and define measurable usability requirements.

Acceptance Criteria
Visual design requirements documented.
Navigation requirements documented.
Accessibility requirements documented.
Stakeholder approval obtained.

tickets.title: Clarify Early-Morning Warning Behavior
tickets.description:
Description
Determine how course time warnings should work and whether they are configurable.

Acceptance Criteria
Warning trigger conditions defined.
User customization options specified.
Warning workflow approved.

tickets.title: Resolve Roadmap Planning vs Live Enrollment Conflict
tickets.description:
Description
Define how long-term planning and live enrollment validation coexist.

Acceptance Criteria
Planning Mode defined.
Registration Mode defined.
Full-course handling documented.
Stakeholder agreement obtained.

tickets.title: US1: Automatic Prerequisite Validation
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want the system to automatically check course prerequisites, so that I can avoid selecting courses that I am not eligible to take.

Acceptance Criteria: 

The system checks prerequisites when a course is selected.
Students cannot add a course if prerequisites are not met.
The system displays the missing prerequisite(s).
The course can be added only when all prerequisites are satisfied.

tickets.title: US2: Drag-and-Drop Academic Roadmap
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to drag and drop courses into my academic roadmap, so that I can easily plan my path to graduation.

Acceptance Criteria: 
Students can drag courses into a semester.
Students can move courses between semesters.
Changes are saved automatically.
The updated roadmap is displayed immediately.

tickets.title: US3: Early-Morning Course Warning
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to receive a warning when selecting an 8:00 AM class, so that I can make a more informed scheduling decision.

Acceptance Criteria: 
A warning message appears when an 8:00 AM course is selected.
The warning is displayed before the course is added.
Students can continue after acknowledging the warning.
The warning does not prevent course selection.

analysis.ambiguity1: "The course catalog is ugly" : This requirement is ambiguous because the term "ugly" is subjective and does not provide measurable criteria. It is unclear whether stakeholders want changes to the visual design, navigation, readability, search functionality, or overall user experience.
analysis.ambiguity2: "I want the app to warn me if I am choosing a class that starts at 8:00 AM because I will definitely oversleep." : This requirement is vague because it is based on an individual preference. It does not specify whether the warning should apply to all students, whether students can customize the warning time, or whether the warning is mandatory or optional.
analysis.conflictSolution:
The Registrar states that students should not be able to add full classes to their roadmap because the system must use live enrollment data. However, the Head of Advising wants students to plan their academic roadmap up to four years in advance, even when future course availability is unknown.
Proposed Solution:
The system should support two planning modes. For current and upcoming semesters, course availability should be validated using live enrollment data and full classes should be clearly marked. For long-term planning, students may add future courses regardless of current availability, but the system should display them as "planned" and indicate that future seat availability is not guaranteed.
analysis.nfr1:
Security
The system must ensure that students can only view and modify their own academic plans and schedules.
analysis.nfr2:
Auditability
The system must record and maintain logs of all advisor prerequisite overrides, including the advisor identity, timestamp, and reason for the override.
analysis.nfr3:
Usability
The system should provide an intuitive drag-and-drop interface that allows students to easily build and visualize their academic roadmap.
